content.cn_text: 今天，我将探讨三种关于智力的早期心理学家观点，
content.en_text: Today, I'm going to consider three series of intelligence early psychologist states,

content.cn_text: 例如，1920年代的斯皮尔曼认为，每个人都有一个单一的总体智力水平，
content.en_text: such as Spearmad in the 1920s, believed that each individual has a single general level

content.cn_text: 被称为智商（IQ）。根据该理论，我们生来就具有一定的智力水平，
content.en_text: of intelligence known as IQ, according to the theory we are born with a certain level

content.cn_text: 这种智力是从父母那里遗传而来的，并且我们的智力在整个
content.en_text: of intelligence, which is inherited from our parents, and our intelligence remains constant

content.cn_text: 一生中保持不变。
content.en_text: throughout our life.

content.cn_text: 因此，我们随时进行的任何智力测试都会得出大致相似的
content.en_text: It follows that any intelligence tests we do at any time will give broadly similar

content.cn_text: 结果。
content.en_text: results.

content.cn_text: 如果这是真的，那就意味着你在让自己
content.en_text: If this is true, it suggests that they are asin the much you can do to make yourself

content.cn_text: 更聪明方面能做的很少，因为它是一种遗传特征。
content.en_text: more intelligent, since it's genetic feature.

content.cn_text: 关于智力遗传起源的大部分证据来自对
content.en_text: Most of the evidence for the genetic origin of the intelligence has come from studies

content.cn_text: 双胞胎的研究，这些研究显示他们在智力上有相当大的相似性。
content.en_text: of twins, which shows considerable similarity in their intelligence.

content.cn_text: 然而，其他心理学家对这项研究的发现有不同的解读。
content.en_text: However, other psychologists have interpreted the findings of this research differently.

content.cn_text: 该理论认为，此类研究中受试者之间智力的相似性
content.en_text: The theory says that similarity in intelligence between the subjects used in such a studies

content.cn_text: 可能来源于环境，而非遗传。
content.en_text: may have an environmental rather than genetic origin.

content.cn_text: 这些心理学家指出，在同一家庭中长大的孩子很可能
content.en_text: These psychologists that point out the children and brought up in the same family are likely

content.cn_text: 拥有非常相似的经历。
content.en_text: to have experiences which are very similar.

content.cn_text: 但一些针对那些搬家并生活在另一种文化中的儿童的研究表明，
content.en_text: But some research into children who have moved home and lived in another culture from

content.cn_text: 几年后，他们的研究结果出现了显著的改善。
content.en_text: the after few years, there was a significant improvement in the results.

content.cn_text: 在1990年代，霍华德·加德纳声称至少有七种独立的智力，
content.en_text: In the 1990s, Harvard Garden claimed that there are at least seven separate intelligence

content.cn_text: 例如语言和逻辑技能，以及至少是传统的智力，例如
content.en_text: such as linguistic and logical skills, and also, at least traditional ones, such as

content.cn_text: 音乐能力。
content.en_text: the category of musical abilities.

content.cn_text: 神经心理学研究表明，不同的协作能力，如言语，可能
content.en_text: Such in neuropsychology suggests that different cooperative abilities such as a speech may

content.cn_text: 彼此部分独立，并由
content.en_text: be partially independent of each other and controlled by different circuits within the

content.cn_text: 大脑中不同的回路控制。
content.en_text: brain.

content.cn_text: 支持这一理论的事实是，有些人在一个特定领域表现出弱点，
content.en_text: Support for this theory comes from the fact that some people show weakness in one particular

content.cn_text: 例如，有些人发现很难辨认他人的面孔。
content.en_text: area, for instance, some find it very difficult to recognise someone's face.

content.cn_text: 而他们可能记住名字没有问题。
content.en_text: Well they may have no problem remembering names.

content.cn_text: 一个人身上这种明显的优势和劣势差异，支持了加德纳的
content.en_text: These apparent variation in strength and weaknesses, within one person, support garden's

content.cn_text: 观点，即智力是多元的而非单一的。
content.en_text: view, and the intelligence is multiple rather than general.

content.cn_text: 如果你培养了节奏感，这不仅可以如你所愿地提高音乐和舞蹈能力，
content.en_text: If you develop a sense of rhythm, this can improve not only music and dance as you expect,

content.cn_text: 还能提高一些看似不相关的领域，例如拼写。
content.en_text: but also areas that aren't obviously linked, spelling for instance.

content.cn_text: 如果你需要牢记化学公式或物理定律，可以把它变成一首歌，
content.en_text: If you need to learn a chemical formula or law of physics by heart, turn it into a song,

content.cn_text: 即使你跑调了也没关系，它会让你更容易
content.en_text: and it doesn't matter at all if you are out of a key, but it will be easier for you to

content.cn_text: 学习。
content.en_text: learn it.

Intelligence Investigation

Theory from Howard Gardner:
• there are at least 7 different types of intelligence, such as linguistic, logical and $36$ skills.
• Various cognitive skills like speech, are regulated by various circuits in the $37$
• Some people are not good at identifying others' $38$
Conclusion
• When a sense of rhythm is developed, you can unexpectedly improve your music, dancing and $39$
• To remember a chemical formular or physical law, the best way is to make it into a $40$
